title: arenaria tetraquetra var. murcica
intro: Arenaria tetraquetra var. murcica, also known as mountain saxifrage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the saxifrage family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of mountain saxifrage include its ability to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as arthritis and wounds. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical creams, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
